biography: Ricke's journey within the Hector Trophée unfolded across two distinct editions, marking his brief but notable presence in this esteemed competition. During his tenure, he immersed himself in the unique format, which demands excellence in both individual skill and strategic pair dynamics.

Throughout his two outings, Ricke engaged with the formidable challenges presented by the Hector Trophée's multi-day structure. While he demonstrated committed play, neither the coveted Hector Trophée nor the prestigious Victor Trophy for individual supremacy found their way into his possession, underscoring the high competitive bar of the event.

With his involvement now concluded, Ricke is considered to have retired from active competition in Hector events, and he will not be among the field for the upcoming Hector Trophée 2026. His participation, though limited in scope, contributed to the rich tapestry of the tournament's developing history.
